name: Dax, the Opportunistic Looter
lines: Even if the world ends, the price of gold doesn't change. Right?
Monsters? They're just big. One blast from this stun baton and they all shut up.
I don't care about the Resistance or that god's corpse. I just need whatever fills my pockets.

friendly.greeting: Oh, a fellow partner? Did you snag anything good? It'd be easier for both of us if we split the sectors.
friendly.farewell: Watch your back. If you die, all those treasures you gathered belong to me.

normal.greeting: Back off, I claimed this sector first. Don't go interfering with my business.
normal.farewell: Scram. There aren't even any scraps left worth taking here.

hostile.greeting: Emptying a living man's pockets is much faster than scavenging from the dead! Hand it all over!
hostile.farewell: Go ahead, try to run! I'll just reclaim it all once those monsters rip you apart anyway!

deathLine: This was... all... mine... there's still... so much... I haven't taken...
description: A looter taking advantage of the depot's collapse to steal valuables. He is obsessed with gathering gold bars and specialized components at any risk.
encounter: Dax: (Wiping a bloodstained gold bar) Damn it, those monsters are tucked into every corner, making my job a real pain! Don't even think about touching my gold bars!
Dax: The Resistance? Thanks to those idiots messing with the corpse, the management team all ran for it. This is the opportunity of a lifetime for me!
Dax: (Clutching his bag tightly) Mika in the control room or Hansen the mechanic might be going crazy, but not me. I'm perfectly sane. The smell of money keeps me sharp!
Dax: Look over there—see those crystals dropped past the tracks? Do you have any idea what those go for on the market? They're more than worth risking a life for!
Dax: Move! There’s an emergency safe in the stationmaster's office up there that I haven't emptied yet. I have to get there before those monsters smash it to pieces!
